Narrative_Meaning: Sevens signify surprises, whether in the sense of conspiracy, illnesses and light hearted fun.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Stag card represents nature in the form of  visceral, animal life. Animals are driven by base desires, the simplest principles, and so the card shows us this, indicating that the events are concerned with an individual's strength and survival, and perhaps more importantly the long term survival of the species. The Stag is usually timid and will avoid threats, but may be quick to fight during the rutting season, and vicious with those antlers. It is this rutting behaviour that sets the Stag apart.

Significator.Plot: The Stag Plot is a natural story, concerned with the normal conflicts of nature, the difficulties of survival, hunting, mating and child-rearing. These can be stories of nature, or children raised by wolves, bears and lions, or they can be coming of age tales, that mark important and natural events in any life.
Significator.Side: When one side is marked with The Stag they are often elevated by the season or story (as the Stag bellows and threatens during the rut). The Stag side may become Monster Facet Embodiments for the duration of the Story usually during the Zenith.
Significator.Character:
The Stag Significator indicates a Character who is physical, athletic and sexual.

They Gain Yin when they practice, train, or exercise.

They Gain Yang when they compete with another, or show off.

They Gain Chi when their accomplishments are recognised.

They gain +1 Success Level on any purely physically athletic action (such as rutting, running, jumping, swimming, punching, kicking, head-butting).
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A figure of raw physical prowess and vitality, possessing a deep connection to their own animal nature. They are competitive and athletic, thriving on physical challenges and the recognition of their strength and grace.
Significator.Scene: The Stag Scene is physical and sometimes hedonistic, there is often a sense of importance or occasion to these Scenes though. They can be important watershed moments for the Characters. Yarn-Tellers can play a Stag card to create a Physical Ordeal (such as a race or chase), or a scene of debauchery (such as a night in a brothel or strip club). They can also use them to mark such events as weddings and coming of age (manhood/womanhood) events.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Reaper you need to offer them something to collect or gain.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story hooks the character by offering what they currently desire (although not perhaps what they really need). This temptation usually includes a taste of what is to come, a kiss from a potential lover, a hit of their addiction to sooth the tremors, a morsel of food for the starving man, but it is not enough to satiate them, yet.
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: The loyalty of a sizable group—a herd or community that provides safety in numbers and a shared sense of purpose.
Yarn.Fray_Description: A Hunt is a High Stakes Complex Motional Chase Ordeal. The PCs may be trying to find food, or they may be the hunted, trying to escape, then again not all hunts are for food, the search for a mate or a place to live can also be hunts. Hunts can involve Snares, Traps and Obstacles, as well as deadly predators and dangerous prey animals.
Yarn.Snag_Description: After the Fray the PCs or their allies are offered temptations. These may be tempting offers to change sides, or actual seductions, by a love interest (or a love interests Rivals) in some cases. When a PC is seduced it is usual that the some thing reveals later that they have slept with the enemy, and may be betrayed

NPC.Description: Flirts, are incredibly common liberated societies, they may be common in more conservative times and places, but must do their best to keep secret their nature. Which is hard, as being flirty exposes them quite well (they wouldn't want a persecuted Hitch too!).

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs get to rest, meanwhile their opponents (often the Dominant Embodiments) are challenged by one of their Rivals.
Yarn.History_Description: The Stag indicates that the history of the current situation is rooted in bloody soil, this may be some old battle, a long gone hunt, or some other slaughter.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Stag indicates that the current situation is literally one of survival, the stakes are high right now, and failure could easily mean death. There is a often a wilderness or urban jungle element to this situation, and very often a pursuit, chase, hunt or other Motional Ordeal.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Stag indicates that the route out of this current situation is via the "Acute Stress Response", or the "Fight-or-Flight Response", the heightened hormones, and physical responses the body makes when hyperaroused are the key to winning the fight (often by intimidation and raw aggression), fleeing successfully, or the other one.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about your own side, or own self. This might reveal your weaknesses or strengths, if you aren't already painfully aware of them.
Revelations.Info_Description: The information is about what something is for, not necessarily what it is, or what is the expected result. For example when a bald super-villain steals a nuclear weapon, and is buying up desert property, the what for in this case would reveal they wish to destabilise fault lines and flood California.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information reaches the character via their senses, normally this is a direct sensing, they see flashes of light, hear shots fired, and smell and taste the gun-smoke. Sometimes all the senses will be involved, other times only one or two. This vector often is used when the sensory pathway is unusual, as most characters are primarily visual and auditory information based, scent, taste, sense of balance, thermoception (our ability to feel heat), etc. are more likely to be carrying the information.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The Alternate information is about the grace of the Embodiment. There is an elegance, beauty, style or poise to the Embodiment that is worth commenting upon, and reveals something about their strength, fitness, or fashion sense that will be important.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Biological details tell us about the biology of a Character or people. They may include genetic markers, or just knowledge about their species or bloodline. The Carracam for example, clone themselves on death, birthing at least 3 larval children that eat their way out of the corpse.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by a large group of animals (or drunken adults) who may pass naturally but may be aggressive if threatened.

Tarot.Normal: Instantly Fix or replace a Prop (Physical Descendant).
Tarot.Crossed: Target drops a random Prop (Physical Descendant).

Ordeal.Stage_Description: A busy market, square or park, a social or professional engagement.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Get to the end of the Ordeal, on your jet-ski, battle-ray, or riding-shark  through all the Checkpoints, before everyone else.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: Something unexpected by both sides occurs, perhaps a third side joins, the train brakes, an earthquake happens, or a warp vortex opens between the combatants. This has the effect of cancelling any accumulated Flowing Success or Ebbing Failure Levels or emotional effects.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A Group of Monsters (Cast Extra), with at least 1 ♥ Monster Facet and that outnumber the party at least 3 to 1.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Stress.Description: Stress makes things harder. Things that we could previously bear with grace become impossible to cope with, and all too often the Stress interferes with our skills and talents. Stress can even make us revel in these hardships, justifying and internalising the failures.
Stress.Rules: All Heart suited Actions (Enigma, Heresy, Jeer, Liberty, Phoenix, and Nature) add Difficulty equal to the Character’s current Stress in Intrepid Facet Sway. If a penalised Action fails the Character may reduce Stress, Strain or Shock by 1 for each Success Level achieved.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can inhibit or push aside the higher mind, revealing the beast underneath. For some the beast is a crazed carnivore, a deadly predator, who has a strong fight response to any stimulus, others revert to fleeing, or freezing in place, we all have base instincts that we repress, sometimes unknowingly. For the beast there is no thought beyond the perception of stimuli and the physical, instinctual, animal response. For some this is a once in a lifetime break, for others it can become a repeatable mental state.
Trauma.Rules: The conscious mind of a Character is shutdown, or on pause, often this is represented in the Psychosocial Action Space by removing the conscious areas of the mind, instead the Character is reduced to instinctive, animalistic behaviours, they may forage and hunt for food, and often retain instinctual tool use (especially with tools they are familar with holding), empathy and occasionally limited language use, especially at understanding others. Characters in this "mode" cannot Gain Stress from any conscious source, such as Personality Hitches, however they are still effected by emotional effects (perhaps, it could argued, more profoundly without rationalization to inhibit the emotional display) and Psych Attacks (although Psych attacks can only move the Character in the Psychosocial Action Space). The animalistic Character can relieve Stress only by Straining, but can Strain any Facet or Annex Die for 1 Stress.

Age.Description: An Age of Plague is occurring. During this age the society is afflicted by some plague, often an abundance of a particular animal, although plagues can also be bacterial or viral in nature. Plagues devastate societies, mice and locust plagues target foods destroying crops and stores, any plague can spread disease, tainting water supplies, or bringing people in close proximity to dead and living animals.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A harrowing time of pestilence and overabundance, where the natural world turns against society. Whether it is an infestation of vermin or the spread of a deadly disease, the era is defined by the struggle to survive amidst a environment that has become toxic and hostile.
Age.Rules: An animal species explodes in population, such that they appear everywhere, this creates a tremendous negative effect upon the society, as too many animals spread disease and damage property and food stores.
